CheckTitle: Detect applications requesting excessive delegated permissions
Description: Checks for application registrations that request more than 10 delegated permissions (Scope type) from Microsoft Graph. Excessive delegated scopes increase the attack surface if a user consents to the application.
Risk: Applications requesting many delegated permissions gain broad access when a user consents. If the application is compromised or malicious, the attacker can leverage all consented scopes to access user data, mail, files, and directory information.

Remediation.Recommendation: Review applications requesting more than 10 delegated permissions. Remove unnecessary scopes and apply least-privilege principles. Use application permissions with admin consent where appropriate instead of broad delegated scopes.

SourceNotes: Excessive permissions increase blast radius of application compromise.
